channel.title: My AI Filtered News
channel.description: Cleaned news feed via Gemini AI

item.title: Inflation surges to highest level in nearly two years as energy costs spike
item.description: Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years. Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.

item.title: 'How are you using AI?' Your therapist should ask you that question, experts argue
item.description: A paper in JAMA Psychiatry says mental health providers should ask if patients are using artificial intelligence chatbots, just as they would ask patients about sleep habits and substance use.

item.title: ‘For Trump, this is seen as an unmitigated failure, this war’
item.description: “For Trump, this is seen as an unmitigated failure, this war,” said Omar Rahman.

item.title: Liverpool boss Slot says club and fans back him before Anfield protests
item.description: Slot says he feels 'complete support' despite mounting pressure after a disastrous season.

item.title: Egypt and Al-Ahly keeper El-Shenawy banned four games for striking referee
item.description: El-Shenawy was incensed ‌after Al-Ahly's ​appeal for a penalty following ​a handball in stoppage time was denied.

item.title: Gaza father searches for his children’s remains in the rubble for years
item.description: A father in the Al Bureij camp in Gaza survived an Israeli airstrike but four of his children died.

item.title: ‘Neither war nor peace’: What Gaza looks like six months into ‘ceasefire’
item.description: Following October 2025 agreement, attacks continue, aid is insufficient and regional conflicts deepen instability.

item.title: Russia suffers ‘record’ soldier casualties as Ukraine ups drone production
item.description: Russian casualties are mounting, recruitment is falling and territorial gains are dwindling.

item.title: ‘Downward mobility is incredibly radicalizing’: The college bargain is broken. What comes next could reshape America
item.description: "Creative, brilliant people are going to wake up this decade and realize the jobs that they thought they were going to have ... are gone."

item.title: Current price of oil as of April 10, 2026
item.description: When oil prices change, it affects your energy costs—and even the price of everyday items. Here’s why.

item.title: US CPI Jumps on Surge in Gas Prices, But Underlying Inflation Is Tamer
item.description: Shoppers in the SoHo neighborhood of New York. Photographer: Michael Nagle/Bloomberg

item.title: SP Group Renews Push for Tata Sons Listing in Open Letter to RBI
item.description: A substantial minority shareholder of Tata Sons Pvt. reiterated its call for a public listing of the Tata Group holding company, as it sought to convince Indian regulators that such a move is essential to unlock value for investors.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: CoreWeave, Docusign, Lumentum, Organon
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:50 a.m. in New York. Its seven-day winning streak, the longest run for the benchmark since October, looks set to be tested as traders prepare for CPI data.

item.title: GQG’s Kersmanc Says Early Big Tech Exit Is Finally Paying Off
item.description: Brian Kersmanc at GQG Partners LLC says a decision to empty his funds of tech companies more than a year ago is finally starting to pay off.

item.title: US Stocks’ Seven-Day Rally Stalls Ahead of CPI, US-Iran Talks
item.description: US equity futures stalled after a seven-day rally as investors weighed whether a fragile truce between Washington and Tehran can hold, and oil headed for its biggest weekly loss in nine months.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Maintains Oil Exports From Key Red Sea Port for Now
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s oil exports via the Red Sea are holding steady for now, as the impact of a drone attack on its vital cross-country pipeline has yet to filter through.

item.title: Defense Stocks Tumble and Builders Rally on Ukraine Peace Hopes
item.description: European defense stocks slumped on Friday as a senior Ukraine official signaled progress toward a peace deal with Russia, while construction firms rose on bets that a ceasefire could trigger a post-war rebuilding boom.

item.title: Oil Swings Before Weekend Talks After Attacks Hit Saudi Capacity
item.description: Oil switched from gains to losses, ahead of weekend talks between Iran and the US that will dictate the path ahead for a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: Bond Traders Cling to Bets on a Fed Rate Cut This Year After CPI
item.description: Bond traders trimmed wagers that the Federal Reserve will lower interest rates once this year after data confirmed that US inflation quickened in March as the Iran war led to higher gasoline prices.

item.title: US CPI Surges in March, as War Sends Gas Prices Higher
item.description: US inflation surged in March by the most in nearly four years as the war with Iran sent gasoline prices skyrocketing. The consumer price index rose 0.9% from February. Michael McKee reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Stocks Rise on In-Line CPI Ahead of US-Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
item.description: The first inflation snapshot of the war-fueled spike in energy costs matched expectations, driving stocks higher ahead of this weekend’s planned talks between the US and Iran.

item.title: Europe Weeks Away From Jet-Fuel Shortage, Airport Group Says
item.description: Europe faces a systemic jet-fuel shortage in three weeks’ time if the Strait of Hormuz remains restricted until then, a regional airport trade association warns.

item.title: Demand for Dollars in Swaps Ebbs Alongside Global Risks
item.description: By at least one metric in the $9.5 trillion foreign-exchange markets, demand for the dollar is ebbing amid the tenuous ceasefire between the US and Iran.

item.title: 独家：海上自卫队将退出日本南极科考船运用
item.description: 【共同社4月10日电】关于在日本南极科学考察活动中运送队员与物资的南极科考船的运用，政府已开始协调，计划随着破冰舰“白濑”号预计在2034年退役，让海上自卫队退出。鉴于中国的军事活动日益活跃等，日本周边的安全保障环境发生变化，海自的

item.title: 经霍尔木兹海峡驶往日本的油轮最近一周“零通行”
item.description: 【共同社4月10日电】从波斯湾经霍尔木兹海峡驶往日本的原油油轮处于中断状态，最近一周（4日至10日）为零艘。共同社分析船舶自动识别系统（AIS）数据得出这一结论。海峡事实上被封锁的影响已显现。封锁前的一年里，周均约有7艘通过。即使恢复通航，

item.title: US inflation soars in March as war on Iran drives economy into uncertainty
item.description:
Prices were up 3.3% over the year, adding to the unpredictability that first came with Trump tariffs

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

US inflation soared in March amid the US-Israel war with Iran, with prices up 0.9% compared to last month and 3.3% over the year, according to new data released Friday.

The spike in the consumer price index (CPI), which measures the price of a basket of goods and services, is the largest in nearly two years and the first official measure of how the conflict has impacted US consumer prices, particularly as Iran blocked the strait of Hormuz, where a fifth of the world’s oil and gas would typically pass through.

Continue reading...

item.title: Domestic abuser jailed in Scotland for killing wife who took her own life
item.description:
Lee Milne sentenced to eight years in landmark case after being found guilty of culpable homicide of Kimberly Milne

A man convicted of killing his wife, who took her own life after repeated domestic abuse, has been jailed for eight years in a case seen as a significant legal milestone.

Kimberly Milne, 28, died when she jumped from a bridge in July 2023. Her estranged husband, Lee Milne, was found guilty of culpable homicide last month after a trial at the high court in Glasgow.

In the UK, Samaritans can be contacted on freephone 116 123 and the domestic abuse helpline is 0808 2000 247. In the US, the suicide prevention lifeline is 988 and the domestic violence hotline is 1-800-799-SAFE (7233). In Australia, the crisis support service Lifeline is on 13 11 14 and the national family violence counselling service is on 1800 737 732. Other international helplines can be found via www.befrienders.org

Continue reading...

item.title: US should judge UK on its actions, not on social media exchanges, defence secretary says
item.description:
Use of UK military bases in Gulf region has been ‘invaluable’ to US military operation in Iran

  • Middle East crisis live – latest updates

The UK’s actions in the Gulf should be the basis for any US judgment of the country’s value rather than Donald Trump’s social media posts, the defence secretary has said.

Speaking at a conference in London, John Healey said the UK’s recent efforts “spoke for themselves”, as Keir Starmer flew home after a trip that included discussing how to keep the strait of Hormuz open with Donald Trump, as well as meeting leaders in Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain.

Continue reading...

item.title: War has given Iran new leverage for nuclear programme, say US former envoys
item.description:
Negotiators of 2015 deal say Tehran has seen how cutting off Hormuz strait can help it counter asymmetry of power

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Former US envoys who dealt with Iran have said that the US-Israeli attack on Iran and Tehran’s subsequent closure of the strait of Hormuz have given Iran new tools and resolve to resist pressure to shutter its nuclear programme.

Two senior negotiators for the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA), the Obama-era agreement to limit Iran’s nuclear uranium enrichment in exchange for sanctions relief, said the Trump administration’s war had handed Iran a coveted weapon by demonstrating its ability to cut off the strait of Hormuz, an economic chokehold that one negotiator said would help Iran “balance the asymmetry of power” with the US.

Continue reading...

item.title: Middle East crisis live: Vance warns Iran not to ‘play’ US as he heads to Pakistan for talks
item.description:
US vice-president leading US delegation in negotiations due to take place in Islamabad on Saturday

  • Netanyahu says there is no ceasefire in Lebanon as Israel launches fresh strikes

The streets of Islamabad are on strict lockdown as Pakistan’s capital prepares to play host to historic negotiations between Iran and the US that have dangled the promise of an end to war that has devastated the Middle East.

Even as the US-Iran ceasefire looked increasingly precarious, amid Israel’s continued bombardment of Lebanon and disputes over the terms of the talks, Pakistani officials insist that the make-or-break peace negotiations will be going ahead over the weekend as planned

Continue reading...

item.title: Peruvians to go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability
item.description:
Soaring crime and corruption top voter concerns in highly unpredictable election with 35 candidates for president

Peruvians go to the polls on Sunday hoping to break a cycle of instability that has produced nine presidents in a decade as well as surging violent crime, corruption scandals and overwhelming distrust in institutions and politicians.

About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congress – all from a ballot sheet measuring nearly half a metre, the longest in the country’s history.

Continue reading...
